cards.text: Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the heavens... So the LORD dispersed them from there over the face of all the earth.
cards.connection: The origin of humanity's dispersion, setting the stage for the settlement of Africa.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the events following the Flood.

cards.text: When the Most High gave to the nations their inheritance, when he separated the children of mankind, he fixed the borders of the peoples...
cards.connection: Affirms that the migration and boundaries of African peoples were divinely ordained.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the sovereign design behind migrations.

cards.text: And he made from one man every nation of mankind to live on all the face of the earth, having determined allotted periods and the boundaries of their dwelling place.
cards.connection: Highlights the biological unity of all Africans and the divine timing of their expansions.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the biological unity and sovereign timing of peoples.

cards.text: The sons of Ham: Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.
cards.connection: Identifies the foundational progenitors of the African continent.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the Table of Nations.

cards.text: Zillah also bore Tubal-cain; he was the forger of all instruments of bronze and iron.
cards.connection: Demonstrates that metallurgy was an ancient technology preserved from before the Flood, challenging evolutionary models of the 'Iron Age'.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the origins of iron-working in Africa.

cards.text: For what can be known about God is plain to them... so they are without excuse.
cards.connection: Explains the widespread belief in a High God across traditional African religions as a preserved memory of the one true God.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the Bantu High God and traditional religion.
